data_in.description: interim/tables/0_original/structure/smiles.tsv.gz — raw SMILES table from gathering layer

data_out.description: interim/tables/1_translated/structure/unique.tsv.gz — deduplicated, sanitised SMILES table with standardised chemical structures

domain_knowledge: SMILES (Simplified Molecular Input Line Entry System) is a linear notation for encoding chemical structures and must be translated to a canonical form to enable deduplication and downstream analysis.
Chemical sanitization removes structurally invalid, incomplete, or malformed SMILES entries before inclusion in curated datasets.
Deduplication is critical to prevent redundant structure-organism associations and to maintain data integrity across 42,166 unique organisms and 231,330+ curated structures.
The LOTUS database aggregates data from 31 initial open databases, requiring standardisation of diverse SMILES representations to a single canonical form.

verify that sanitization and translation applied by smiles.py and sanitizing.py produce chemically valid and standardized structures
verify that unique.tsv.gz contains only unique structural identifiers with no unintended duplicates after deduplication
verify that the transformation preserves chemical validity and does not introduce structural artifacts or loss of stereochemical information except where expected

inputs: interim/tables/0_original/structure/smiles.tsv.gz — raw SMILES table from gathering layer
methodology_summary: Load raw SMILES table from the gathering layer output.
Translate and standardise SMILES strings using smiles.py to enforce canonical representation.
Validate and sanitise chemical structures using sanitizing.py, removing invalid or malformed entries.
Deduplicate the sanitised structure table to generate the final unique SMILES identifier set.
Validation: output file exists, contains deduplicated SMILES entries, and maintains row integrity across structure-organism mapping.
missing_information: No changelog documenting updates, bug fixes, or version history for the smiles.py and sanitizing.py scripts.

task_description: Convert raw SMILES identifiers from the gathering layer into a sanitised, standardised unique structure table by applying SMILES translation and chemical sanitization. Produce interim/tables/1_translated/structure/unique.tsv.gz as the canonical output.

task_inputs.label: raw SMILES table from gathering layer

task_objective: Implement the smiles.py and sanitizing.py transformation pipeline to convert raw SMILES into deduplicated, standardized structural identifiers.
title: Reconstruct the SMILES-to-InChI structure standardisation step in the curation pipeline

workflow_description: 1. Load raw SMILES table from interim/tables/0_original/structure/smiles.tsv.gz using Python. 2. Execute smiles.py to translate and standardise SMILES strings, generating interim/tables/1_translated/structure/smiles.tsv.gz. 3. Load translated SMILES and apply sanitizing.py to validate chemical structures, remove invalid entries, and deduplicate to generate interim/tables/1_translated/structure/unique.tsv.gz.
